run.output:
Q1 result count: 4 rows
Execution time: 739.98 ms

validation.comparison_result: 3 row(s) differ - small precision errors in sum_disc_price and sum_charge (differences in last 2 decimal places). These are floating-point accumulation errors from order-dependent parallel aggregation.

Q1.notes: Results are functionally correct (4 groups) but have minor precision errors. Performance is 15.4x slower than expected (48ms baseline). Major bottleneck: inefficient aggregate computation in hot loop.

Q1.bottleneck: Precision errors in aggregate computation + 15.4x performance degradation vs baseline (48ms)
Q1.root_cause: 1) Double-precision arithmetic in hot loop causing floating-point accumulation errors due to non-deterministic parallel aggregation order. 2) HDD I/O bottleneck (740ms with MADV_SEQUENTIAL|WILLNEED on 60M rows scanning 7 columns ~2.5GB data). 3) AVX2 SIMD instead of AVX-512 - only processing 8 int32s per instruction instead of 16. 4) Inefficient aggregate math: double multiplications/divisions per row in hot loop. 5) std::map for aggregation instead of pre-sized flat hash table.

analysis.overall: Q1 has critical precision errors and severe performance degradation (15.4x slower than 48ms baseline). The filter selectivity is 98.8% (59M rows → 58M passing), so this is effectively a full scan + aggregation workload. On HDD with 64 cores and AVX-512, we should achieve ~50-100ms. Current 740ms indicates major I/O and computation inefficiencies.

critical_fixes.issue: Floating-point precision errors in sum_disc_price and sum_charge
critical_fixes.fix: Change aggregate computation to use fixed-point integer arithmetic instead of double-precision floating-point. Accumulate in int64_t (cents already), compute disc_price and charge in cents using integer math: disc_price = price * (100 - disc) / 100, charge = disc_price * (100 + tax) / 100. This eliminates non-deterministic parallel aggregation errors.
critical_fixes.description: Lines 266-267, 303-304: Replace double disc_price and charge with int64_t fixed-point computation. Change AggValue.sum_disc_price and sum_charge to int64_t. Only convert to double at final output (lines 388-391).

performance_optimizations.technique: Optimized mmap I/O hints for HDD
performance_optimizations.description: Change madvise from MADV_SEQUENTIAL|WILLNEED (line 120) to just MADV_SEQUENTIAL. WILLNEED triggers immediate prefetch of entire 2.5GB dataset, causing HDD thrashing. For HDD, rely on sequential read-ahead. Additionally, consider using MAP_POPULATE only for small hot columns (returnflag, linestatus) or removing WILLNEED entirely to let kernel manage I/O pacing.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 2-3x speedup (740ms → 250-370ms). WILLNEED on HDD with large dataset (2.5GB) causes all columns to compete for disk bandwidth simultaneously, saturating HDD IOPS. Sequential-only allows kernel to stream data efficiently. DuckDB likely streams data more efficiently.

performance_optimizations.description: Replace AVX2 (_mm256) with AVX-512 (_mm512) for date filtering (lines 242-283). Current code processes 8 int32s per instruction; AVX-512 can process 16. Hardware has avx512f, avx512dq, avx512bw, avx512vl. Use _mm512_loadu_si512, _mm512_cmpgt_epi32_mask, _mm512_mask_compressstoreu_epi32 for branchless filtering.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.3-1.5x speedup on CPU-bound phase after I/O fix (reduces filter time by ~40%). Combined with I/O fix: 740ms → 170-280ms.

performance_optimizations.technique: Pre-sized flat hash table for aggregation
performance_optimizations.description: Replace std::map<AggKey, AggValue> (lines 223, 333) with pre-sized flat hash table. Q1 has 4 groups (returnflag: 3 values, linestatus: 2 values → 3×2=6 max groups). Use perfect hashing: index = returnflag * 2 + linestatus, direct array access. Define: AggValue local_results[num_threads][6]. Eliminates hash table overhead, improves cache locality, enables SIMD-friendly layout.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.2-1.4x speedup on aggregation (reduces aggregation overhead by 30-40%). After I/O and SIMD fixes: 740ms → 120-230ms total. This brings us to DuckDB performance range (87-150ms).

performance_optimizations.description: Current code does 2 multiplications and 2 divisions per row (lines 266-267, 303-304). Pre-compute division by 100.0 as multiplication by 0.01 constant. Use FMA instructions if available (_mm_fmadd_pd). Better: switch to fixed-point integer math (see critical_fix) to avoid FP entirely. If keeping double, hoist division: const double inv100 = 0.01; disc_price = price * (100 - disc) * inv100;
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.1-1.2x speedup (minor, but every bit helps). Combined with all above: 740ms → 100-200ms.

performance_optimizations.description: On HDD, sequential reads are critical. Current code opens all 7 columns (lines 184-211), triggering parallel seeks. For HDD, open columns in on-disk order, or better: use single interleaved file if possible (not supported by current storage). Alternative: prefetch only l_shipdate first, filter to selection vector, then load other columns only for matching rows (late materialization).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.2-1.5x speedup on HDD by reducing seeks (740ms → 500-600ms if combined with WILLNEED removal). Late materialization is more complex but could reach 2x if filter is very selective (not the case here: 98.8% pass rate).

performance_optimizations.description: Current code uses atomic range_idx with single scan range [0, 60M) (line 224). This creates contention. Pre-split into num_threads ranges: each thread gets [i*chunk, (i+1)*chunk) without atomics. Morsel size 50K is good for L3 cache. Change to: pre-split scan_ranges into 64 chunks of ~937K rows each, process with static assignment.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 1.1-1.15x speedup by reducing atomic contention (minor but measurable on 64 threads). Combined with all optimizations: 740ms → 90-180ms.

summary: Q1 iteration 0 has critical floating-point precision errors requiring fixed-point integer arithmetic. Performance is 15.4x slower than baseline (740ms vs 48ms). Primary bottleneck: HDD I/O with MADV_WILLNEED causing 2.5GB prefetch + thrashing (2-3x slowdown). Secondary: AVX2 instead of AVX-512 (1.5x), std::map aggregation (1.3x), inefficient aggregate math (1.2x). Recommendation: Fix precision errors first (non-negotiable), then apply I/O optimization (MADV_SEQUENTIAL only) for 2-3x gain, then AVX-512 + perfect hash aggregation to reach 100-200ms (2-4x faster than DuckDB's 87-150ms).
